<a id="type-com-daml-ledger-javaapi-data-codegen-contractcompanion"></a>

## `com.daml.ledger.javaapi.data.codegen.ContractCompanion`

- Introduced: `3.4.10`
- Deprecated: -
- Removed: -

Upstream docs: [Open](https://javadoc.io/doc/com.daml/bindings-java/3.4.11/com/daml/ledger/javaapi/data/codegen/ContractCompanion.html)

**Signature**

```text
public abstract class ContractCompanion<Ct,Id,Data> extends ContractTypeCompanion<Ct,Id,Data,Data>
```

**Summary**

Metadata and utilities associated with a template as a whole, rather than one single contract made from that template. Application code should not instantiate or subclass; instead, refer to the COMPANION field on generated Template subclasses. All protected members herein are considered part of the INTERNAL API. Every instance is either a ContractCompanion.WithKey or ContractCompanion.WithoutKey, depending on whether the template defined a key type. ContractCompanion.WithKey defines extra utilities for working with contract keys.

**Members**

| [Open](https://javadoc.io/doc/com.daml/bindings-java/3.4.11/com/daml/ledger/javaapi/data/codegen/ContractCompanion.html#valueDecoder(com.daml.ledger.javaapi.data.codegen.ContractCompanion)) | `valueDecoder(ContractCompanion<?, ? extends ContractId<Data>, Data>)` | `3.4.10` | - | - |

Upstream docs: [Open](https://javadoc.io/doc/com.daml/bindings-java/3.4.11/com/daml/ledger/javaapi/data/codegen/ContractId.html)

**Signature**

```text
public class ContractId<T> extends Object implements Exercises<ExerciseCommand>
```

**Summary**

This class is used as a super class for all concrete ContractIds generated by the java codegen with the following properties: Foo.ContractId fooCid = new Foo.ContractId("test"); Bar.ContractId barCid = new Bar.ContractId("test"); ContractId&lt;Foo&gt; genericFooCid = new ContractId&lt;&gt;("test"); ContractId&lt;Foo&gt; genericBarCid = new ContractId&lt;&gt;("test"); fooCid.equals(genericFooCid) == true; genericFooCid.equals(fooCid) == true; fooCid.equals(barCid) == false; barCid.equals(fooCid) == false; Due to erase, we cannot distinguish ContractId&lt;Foo&gt; from ContractId&lt;Bar&gt;, thus: fooCid.equals(genericBarCid) == true genericBarCid.equals(fooCid) == true genericFooCid.equals(genericBarCid) == true genericBarCid.equals(genericFooCid) == true


Base class of all decoded-to-codegen Daml records with no type parameters. This category includes all Template payloads, all interface views, and [by convention albeit not by rule] all choice arguments. Its encoded counterpart is DamlRecord, which can be produced with toValue().

<a id="type-com-daml-ledger-javaapi-data-codegen-exercises-archivable"></a>

## `com.daml.ledger.javaapi.data.codegen.Exercises.Archivable`

- Introduced: `3.4.10`
- Deprecated: -
- Removed: -

Upstream docs: [Open](https://javadoc.io/doc/com.daml/bindings-java/3.4.11/com/daml/ledger/javaapi/data/codegen/Exercises.Archivable.html)

**Signature**

```text
public static interface Exercises.Archivable<Cmd> extends Exercises<Cmd>
```

**Summary**

Adds exerciseArchive() to every exercise target. The goal is to correct the problem of having ContractId implement Exercises.makeExerciseCmd(com.daml.ledger.javaapi.data.codegen.Choice&lt;?, ? super A, R&gt;, A) directly. That was a mistake, but one at least obviated by the fact that Exercises.makeExerciseCmd(com.daml.ledger.javaapi.data.codegen.Choice&lt;?, ? super A, R&gt;, A) is clearly part of the internal API, so if you use it directly anyway and you get weird exceptions, you get to keep both pieces of your program. With exerciseArchive() we can correct the problem by having Exercises.Archivable be the real Exercises interface, and the distinction can be flattened when breaking compatibility.

**Members**

| Docs | Member | Introduced | Deprecated | Removed |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [Open](https://javadoc.io/doc/com.daml/bindings-java/3.4.11/com/daml/ledger/javaapi/data/codegen/Exercises.Archivable.html#exerciseArchive%28%29) | `exerciseArchive()` | `3.4.10` | - | - |

<a id="type-com-daml-ledger-javaapi-data-codegen-valuedecoder"></a>

## `com.daml.ledger.javaapi.data.codegen.ValueDecoder`

- Introduced: `3.4.10`
- Deprecated: -
- Removed: -

Upstream docs: [Open](https://javadoc.io/doc/com.daml/bindings-java/3.4.11/com/daml/ledger/javaapi/data/codegen/ValueDecoder.html)

**Signature**

```text
@FunctionalInterface public interface ValueDecoder<Data>
```

**Summary**

A converter from the encoded form of a Daml value, represented by Value, to the codegen-decoded form, represented by Data. Every codegen class for a template, record, or variant includes a valueDecoder method that produces one of these. If the data type has type parameters, valueDecoder has arguments that correspond to ValueDecoders for those type arguments. For primitive types that are not code-generated, see PrimitiveValueDecoders. // given template 'Foo', and encoded payload 'Value fooValue' Foo foo = Foo.valueDecoder().decode(fooValue); // given Daml datatypes 'Bar a b' and 'Baz', // and encoded 'Bar' 'Value barValue' Bar&lt;Baz, Long&gt; bar = Bar.valueDecoder( Baz.valueDecoder(), PrimitiveValueDecoders.fromInt64) .decode(barValue); Bar&lt;List&lt;Baz&gt;, Map&lt;Long, String&gt;&gt; barWithAggregates = Bar.valueDecoder( PrimitiveValueDecoders.fromList(Baz.valueDecoder), PrimitiveValueDecoders.fromGenMap( PrimitiveValueDecoders.fromInt64, PrimitiveValueDecoders.fromText)) .decode(barAggregateValue);

**Members**

| Docs | Member | Introduced | Deprecated | Removed |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [Open](https://javadoc.io/doc/com.daml/bindings-java/3.4.11/com/daml/ledger/javaapi/data/codegen/ValueDecoder.html#decode(com.daml.ledger.javaapi.data.Value)) | `decode(Value)` | `3.4.10` | - | - |

## `com.daml.ledger.javaapi.data.codegen.Variant`

- Introduced: `3.4.10`
- Deprecated: -
- Removed: -

Upstream docs: [Open](https://javadoc.io/doc/com.daml/bindings-java/3.4.11/com/daml/ledger/javaapi/data/codegen/Variant.html)

**Signature**

```text
public abstract class Variant<T> extends Object implements DefinedDataType<T>
```

**Summary**

Base class of all decoded-to-codegen Daml variants with no type parameters. Its encoded counterpart is Variant, which can be produced with toValue().
